generated_comment: Arre Neha, sun toh sahi! Mummy-Papa everyday call kar rahe the, 'Neha serious nahi hai, tum dekho.' Look, I know you're upset, but you're 28 now. This isn't like college where you can just chill. The good matches, the boys with good families and careers, they look for younger girls. You don't have the luxury to say 'abhi time hai' anymore. Shaadi kab karegi phir?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic as it reinforces gendered expectations around marriage age for women, implying a diminishing 'market value' for a bride after a certain age ('good matches look for younger girls'). It frames her age as a constraint that removes her 'luxury' of choice, pressuring her into marriage based on societal norms rather than her own timeline.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Delhi. You are on a matrimonial website helping your younger sister Neha who is 28 create a profile because your parents have been asking you to handle it since Neha is not taking it seriously herself. You are on a phone call with Neha telling her you have created the profile and what you have written and what filters you have set for prospective matches. Neha is not happy that you did this without asking her. You are telling her why you did it anyway and why at 28 she does not have the luxury of not taking this seriously
